description: Service Catalog Backend - Custom mediator, EF Core + PostgreSQL setup, and full CRUD for Service and ServiceCategory

userStories.title: Implement custom mediator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the custom mediator infrastructure so that all feature slices can use the CQRS pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: IRequest<TResponse> marker interface exists in Chairly.Api/Shared/Mediator/
IRequestHandler<TRequest, TResponse> interface exists in Chairly.Api/Shared/Mediator/
IMediator interface and Mediator implementation exist — dispatches requests to the correct handler based on request type
IPipelineBehavior<TRequest, TResponse> interface exists for cross-cutting concerns

ValidationBehavior<TRequest, TResponse> pipeline behavior validates requests using Data Annotations before the handler runs — if validation fails, return a ValidationProblemDetails-style error (do not throw exceptions)
AddMediator(this IServiceCollection) extension method registers all handlers and pipeline behaviors via assembly scanning
Mediator is registered in Program.cs
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation for all feature slices. See ADR-005 for the mediator pattern. No MediatR NuGet package — custom implementation only.

userStories.title: Set up EF Core with PostgreSQL and hardcoded tenant
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database infrastructure so that entities can be persisted to PostgreSQL.
acceptanceCriteria: Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL and Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore packages added to Chairly.Infrastructure
Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design package added to Chairly.Api (for migrations CLI)
ChairlyDbContext created in Chairly.Infrastructure/Persistence/ inheriting DbContext
DbContext is registered in Program.cs with the PostgreSQL connection string from appsettings.Development.json
A hardcoded default TenantId constant exists in Chairly.Api/Shared/Tenancy/TenantConstants.cs with a TODO comment: Replace with tenant resolution middleware (ADR-007)
Connection string added to appsettings.Development.json under ConnectionStrings:ChairlyDb pointing to Host=localhost;Database=chairly_dev;Username=postgres;Password=postgres
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: See ADR-003 for EF Core conventions and ADR-007 for database-per-tenant strategy. For now we use a single hardcoded tenant.

userStories.title: Create ServiceCategory and Service domain entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the domain entities so that the service catalog data can be modeled.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceCategory entity in Chairly.Domain/Entities/ with properties: Id (Guid), TenantId (Guid), Name (string), SortOrder (int)

Service entity in Chairly.Domain/Entities/ with properties: Id (Guid), TenantId (Guid), Name (string), Description (string, nullable), Duration (TimeSpan), Price (decimal), CategoryId (Guid, nullable), IsActive (bool), SortOrder (int), CreatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset), UpdatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset, nullable)
Service has a navigation property to ServiceCategory (optional relationship)
No EF Core attributes on domain entities — configuration is done in Infrastructure
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: See docs/domain-model.md section 4 (Services) for the full entity definitions. Domain project has no EF Core dependency.

userStories.title: Add EF Core configurations and initial migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need EF Core entity configurations and an initial migration so that the database schema is created.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceCategoryConfiguration in Chairly.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/ implementing IEntityTypeConfiguration<ServiceCategory>
ServiceConfiguration in Chairly.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/ implementing IEntityTypeConfiguration<Service>
Service.Name has a max length and is required
Service.Name + TenantId has a unique index (service name unique within tenant)
ServiceCategory.Name has a max length and is required
Service.Price is configured with precision(10, 2)
Service.CategoryId is an optional FK to ServiceCategory with SetNull on delete
DbSet<Service> and DbSet<ServiceCategory> added to ChairlyDbContext
Initial EF Core migration created via dotnet ef migrations add
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: See ADR-003 for EF Core conventions. Use separate IEntityTypeConfiguration<T> classes. The migration startup project is Chairly.Api, the DbContext project is Chairly.Infrastructure.

userStories.title: Create ServiceCategory CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to manage service categories so that services can be organized into groups.
acceptanceCriteria: CreateServiceCategory/ slice in Chairly.Api/Features/Services/CreateServiceCategory/: command with Name (required) and SortOrder (int), handler creates entity with hardcoded TenantId, endpoint POST /api/service-categories returns 201 with created entity
GetServiceCategoriesList/ slice: query returns all categories for the hardcoded tenant ordered by SortOrder, endpoint GET /api/service-categories returns 200
UpdateServiceCategory/ slice: command with Id, Name, SortOrder, handler updates entity, endpoint PUT /api/service-categories/{id} returns 200 with updated entity or 404 if not found
DeleteServiceCategory/ slice: command with Id, handler deletes entity, endpoint DELETE /api/service-categories/{id} returns 204 or 404 if not found
All commands have Data Annotations validation (e.g. [Required] on Name, [MaxLength])
Endpoints are mapped in a ServiceCategoryEndpoints extension method and registered in Program.cs
Handlers use OneOf for result pattern — e.g. OneOf<ServiceCategoryResponse, NotFound> for update/delete
Response DTOs are used — never expose domain entities directly
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: See ADR-005 for slice structure. Each slice has its own folder under Features/Services/. Use Minimal APIs with route groups. ServiceCategory is simpler than Service — no timestamps beyond what's implicit.

userStories.title: Create Service CRUD endpoints (Create, Get, List)
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to create services and view them so that the salon catalog can be built.
acceptanceCriteria: CreateService/ slice: command with Name (required), Description (optional), Duration (required, TimeSpan), Price (required, decimal >= 0), CategoryId (optional Guid), SortOrder (int), handler creates entity with IsActive = true and hardcoded TenantId, endpoint POST /api/services returns 201
GetService/ slice: query by Id, endpoint GET /api/services/{id} returns 200 with service details including category name if set, or 404 if not found
GetServicesList/ slice: query returns all services for the hardcoded tenant ordered by SortOrder, includes category name, endpoint GET /api/services returns 200
All commands have Data Annotations validation
Handlers use OneOf for result pattern
Response DTOs include category name when a category is assigned
Endpoints are mapped in a ServiceEndpoints extension method and registered in Program.cs
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: This story covers Create + Read operations. Update and Delete are in the next story. Include category name in responses by joining/loading the ServiceCategory navigation property.

userStories.title: Create Service Update, Delete, and Toggle Active endpoints
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to update, delete, and toggle active status on services so that the catalog can be maintained.
acceptanceCriteria: UpdateService/ slice: command with all editable fields, handler updates entity and sets UpdatedAtUtc to DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, endpoint PUT /api/services/{id} returns 200 or 404 if not found
DeleteService/ slice: command with Id, handler deletes entity, endpoint DELETE /api/services/{id} returns 204 or 404 if not found
ToggleServiceActive/ slice: command with Id, handler toggles IsActive and sets UpdatedAtUtc, endpoint PATCH /api/services/{id}/toggle-active returns 200 with updated service or 404 if not found
All commands have Data Annotations validation where applicable
Handlers use OneOf for result pattern
All new endpoints are added to the existing ServiceEndpoints extension method
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: This continues the ServiceEndpoints from SC-006. UpdatedAtUtc should be set to DateTimeOffset.UtcNow in Update and ToggleActive handlers.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for ServiceCategory handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests to verify business logic in the ServiceCategory handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests for CreateServiceCategoryHandler — happy path creates category and returns response
Unit tests for CreateServiceCategoryHandler — validation failure (empty name)
Unit tests for UpdateServiceCategoryHandler — happy path updates and returns response
Unit tests for UpdateServiceCategoryHandler — returns NotFound for non-existent id
Unit tests for DeleteServiceCategoryHandler — happy path deletes and returns success
Unit tests for DeleteServiceCategoryHandler — returns NotFound for non-existent id
Tests use an in-memory database or SQLite provider for the DbContext
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Test handlers directly by instantiating them with a test DbContext. Use in-memory SQLite or EF Core InMemory provider. Add Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory or Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite to Chairly.Tests if needed.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for Service handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests to verify business logic in the Service handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests for CreateServiceHandler — happy path creates service with IsActive = true
Unit tests for CreateServiceHandler — validation failure (empty name, negative price)
Unit tests for UpdateServiceHandler — happy path updates and sets UpdatedAtUtc
Unit tests for UpdateServiceHandler — returns NotFound for non-existent id
Unit tests for DeleteServiceHandler — happy path deletes and returns success
Unit tests for DeleteServiceHandler — returns NotFound for non-existent id
Unit tests for ToggleServiceActiveHandler — toggles true to false and false to true
Unit tests for ToggleServiceActiveHandler — returns NotFound for non-existent id
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Same test infrastructure as SC-008. Test the toggle handler both directions (active -> inactive and inactive -> active).

userStories.title: Fix TenantId filtering on all single-entity lookups
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all single-entity lookups to filter by TenantId to prevent cross-tenant data access.
acceptanceCriteria: UpdateServiceCategoryHandler filters by TenantId: FirstOrDefaultAsync predicate includes sc.TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId
DeleteServiceCategoryHandler filters by TenantId: FirstOrDefaultAsync predicate includes sc.TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId
GetServiceHandler filters by TenantId: FirstOrDefaultAsync predicate includes s.TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId
UpdateServiceHandler filters by TenantId: FirstOrDefaultAsync predicate includes s.TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId
DeleteServiceHandler filters by TenantId: FirstOrDefaultAsync predicate includes s.TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId
ToggleServiceActiveHandler filters by TenantId: FirstOrDefaultAsync predicate includes s.TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — Security bug. All single-entity lookups query by Id only without TenantId, allowing cross-tenant access. Add && entity.TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId to every FirstOrDefaultAsync predicate in these 6 handlers.

userStories.title: Add missing audit fields to entities and update migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CreatedBy/UpdatedBy fields on Service and CreatedAtUtc/CreatedBy on ServiceCategory per CLAUDE.md conventions (ADR-009).
acceptanceCriteria: Service entity gains CreatedBy (Guid) and UpdatedBy (Guid?, nullable) properties
ServiceCategory entity gains CreatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset) and CreatedBy (Guid) properties
ServiceConfiguration updated to configure CreatedBy and UpdatedBy columns
ServiceCategoryConfiguration updated to configure CreatedAtUtc and CreatedBy columns
A new EF Core migration is created for the added columns
CreateServiceHandler sets CreatedBy to Guid.Empty (no auth yet) with a TODO comment
UpdateServiceHandler and ToggleServiceActiveHandler set UpdatedBy to Guid.Empty with a TODO comment
CreateServiceCategoryHandler sets CreatedAtUtc and CreatedBy on the new entity
ServiceResponse DTO updated to include CreatedBy and UpdatedBy
ServiceCategoryResponse DTO updated to include CreatedAtUtc and CreatedBy
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — CLAUDE.md requires CreatedAtUtc/CreatedBy on all entities and {Action}AtUtc/{Action}By pairs per ADR-009. Since there is no auth yet, set By fields to Guid.Empty with a TODO comment referencing Keycloak integration.

userStories.title: Fix mediator namespace and add TODO to ValidationBehavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the mediator namespace to match the folder structure and a TODO on ValidationBehavior documenting the exception trade-off.
acceptanceCriteria: All files in Chairly.Api/Shared/Mediator/ use namespace Chairly.Api.Shared.Mediator (not Chairly.Api.Dispatching)
All using statements referencing Chairly.Api.Dispatching across the codebase are updated to Chairly.Api.Shared.Mediator
ValidationBehavior has a TODO comment: Consider returning validation errors via OneOf instead of exceptions (see CLAUDE.md no-exceptions rule)
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — Convention issue. Namespace should match folder path. Also document the ValidationBehavior exception trade-off with a TODO.

userStories.title: Remove TenantId from response DTOs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to stop exposing TenantId in API responses since the tenant is implicit from the request context.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceCategoryResponse no longer includes TenantId
ServiceResponse no longer includes TenantId
All handler code that constructs these DTOs is updated to remove the TenantId argument
All tests that reference TenantId in response assertions are updated
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — Convention issue. TenantId is an internal implementation detail and should not be leaked to API consumers.

userStories.title: Fix line endings and add tests for query handlers and tenant filtering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all files to use LF line endings, and I need test coverage for query handlers and tenant-related assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: Run dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx to fix all line ending issues across the codebase
Add happy-path unit test for GetServiceHandler — returns service with category name
Add happy-path unit test for GetServicesListHandler — returns list ordered by SortOrder
Add happy-path unit test for GetServiceCategoriesListHandler — returns list ordered by SortOrder
Add test asserting CreateServiceHandler assigns TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId to the created entity
Add test asserting CreateServiceCategoryHandler assigns TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId to the created entity
ToggleServiceActiveHandler_TogglesFromInactiveToActive test asserts UpdatedAtUtc is not null
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — Fix CRLF line endings (run dotnet format), add missing query handler tests, add TenantId assertions on create tests, and fix the missing UpdatedAtUtc assertion on the inactive-to-active toggle test.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for the custom mediator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the custom mediator infrastructure to verify dispatching, pipeline behavior execution, and validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Chairly.Tests/Shared/Mediator/MediatorTests.cs with tests for the Mediator class
Test that Mediator.Send dispatches to the correct IRequestHandler and returns the handler's result
Test that Mediator.Send throws InvalidOperationException when no handler is registered for a request type
Test that pipeline behaviors execute in registration order (before and after the handler)
Test that ValidationBehavior rejects a request with invalid Data Annotations (e.g. missing [Required] field) and throws ValidationException with correct error details
Test that ValidationBehavior passes through a valid request to the handler without errors
Test that multiple pipeline behaviors are chained correctly (e.g. validation runs before a second behavior)
Tests use real DI container (ServiceCollection) with AddMediator() to verify assembly scanning and registration
All tests pass: dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — The custom mediator is core infrastructure with zero test coverage. Test the Mediator class, ValidationBehavior, and the AddMediator DI registration. Use test doubles (fake handlers/behaviors) to isolate mediator logic.

userStories.title: Add Scalar API documentation UI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an interactive API documentation UI so that I can test endpoints without external tools like Postman.
acceptanceCriteria: Install Scalar.AspNetCore NuGet package in Chairly.Api
OpenAPI/Swagger document generation is configured in Program.cs (AddOpenApi or equivalent)
Scalar UI is mapped in Program.cs with app.MapScalarApiReference() at /scalar (or /api-docs)
Scalar UI is only enabled in Development environment (inside if (app.Environment.IsDevelopment()) block)
All existing endpoints are visible and testable in the Scalar UI
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — Developers need a way to test API endpoints without Postman. Scalar is a modern, beautiful OpenAPI UI. In .NET 10, use builder.Services.AddOpenApi() and app.MapOpenApi() for the OpenAPI spec, then app.MapScalarApiReference() for the UI.

userStories.title: Configure code coverage enforcement with minimum threshold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need code coverage collection and a minimum coverage threshold enforced on test runs so that test quality is maintained.
acceptanceCriteria: Install coverlet.collector NuGet package in Chairly.Tests (if not already present)
Install coverlet.msbuild NuGet package in Chairly.Tests for threshold enforcement
Configure Chairly.Tests.csproj with coverlet MSBuild properties: CollectCoverage=true, CoverletOutputFormat=cobertura, Threshold=80, ThresholdType=line, ThresholdStat=total
Exclude test projects, migrations, and auto-generated code from coverage via ExcludeByFile or ExcludeByAttribute (e.g. [ExcludeFromCodeCoverage], Migrations folder)
Exclude Program.cs (composition root) and EF Core migration files from coverage calculation
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx fails if line coverage drops below 80%
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes with current test suite (current coverage must be >= 80%)
Update the backend CI workflow (.github/workflows/backend-ci.yml) to include --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" in the dotnet test command
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: REVIEW FIX — No guardrails exist for code coverage. Use coverlet.msbuild for threshold enforcement (fails the build if coverage drops below 80% line coverage). Exclude infrastructure code (migrations, Program.cs) from the calculation. The threshold should be achievable with the current test suite — if not, adjust to the current level and leave a TODO to increase it.
